name: Transform voice transcripts to LinkedIn posts with Claude AI and email automation

messageValues.message: =You are a LinkedIn content expert helping create thought leadership posts. You will receive inspiration content and create engaging LinkedIn posts with accompanying image/video descriptions.

=EXAMPLE POSTS FOR STYLE REFERENCE:
{{ $json.data[0] }}

---

CREATE A NEW POST BASED ON THIS INSPIRATION:
{{ $json.Content[0] }}

Requirements:
1. Match the style and tone of the example posts above
2. Write an engaging LinkedIn post (150-300 words)
3. Create a description for complementary visual content that will be used to provide creative direction to a graphic designer that has our brand guidelines

You must respond with ONLY valid JSON in this exact format. Use only standard ASCII characters, no special Unicode formatting:

{
  "post_content": "Your LinkedIn post here",
  "image_description": "Detailed description of visual content needed"
}

Do not use any special Unicode characters, mathematical styling, or fancy formatting. Use plain text only.

parameters.content:
## Configure Email 
Configure Email Monitoring

Set up dedicated email address for receiving your voice transcriptions
• Create a new email specifically for this workflow (e.g., linkedin@yourdomain.com)
• This keeps your workflow organized and secure


Configure IMAP Email Node
• Add your email credentials in the "Email to Monitor" node
• Set up IMAP access in your email provider settings
• Test the connection to ensure emails are being received


Set Email Filters
• Update the "Limit email sender" nodes with YOUR email address
• Replace "email@email.com" with your sending email
• This ensures only YOUR emails trigger the workflow

Email Usage Instructions
• Record voice memo on your phone
• Copy the auto-transcription from your voice recorder app
• Email the transcription to your dedicated workflow email
• The workflow will automatically trigger when your email is received

## Data Processing Steps

Clean JSON Node
• Extracts email content and metadata
Removes unnecessary email headers and formatting
• Prepares clean text for AI processing

Google Doc Inspiration Source
• CRITICAL: Create your own google doc, and set its permissions to Public, so the URL is publicly accessible.
• Replace with your own Google Doc URL containing example posts
• Document should include posts you admire from other creators. Make sure you copy/paste the text here.
• Keep this document updated with new inspiring content
• This step will reformat your raw, unedited text into a structure similar to your inspiration posts.


Content Merging
• The workflow combines your raw transcription with inspiration examples
• This ensures consistent brand voice and messaging structure



Setting Up Your Inspiration Document
• Create a Google Doc with 10-15 example LinkedIn posts
• Include posts that match your desired tone and style
• Make the document publicly viewable or shareable via link
• Update the URL in the HTTP Request node

## AI Configuration

Anthropic API Setup
• Sign up for Anthropic Claude API access
• Add your API credentials to the "Anthropic Chat Model" node
The workflow uses Claude Sonnet 4 for content generation

Prompt Engineering
• The LLM Chain uses your inspiration posts as style examples
• Generates two outputs: LinkedIn post content + image description
• Maintains 150-300 word count for optimal LinkedIn engagement


JSON Response Cleaning
• Automated parsing extracts clean content from AI response
• Handles formatting issues and ensures proper data structure
• Error handling for incomplete or malformed responses



AI Output Quality Tips
• Keep your inspiration document updated with high-quality examples
• The AI will match the tone and style of your example posts
• More diverse examples = more varied but consistent output

## Email yourself

Connect your email, and email yourself the polished content. 

You'll receive the post content + inspiration for any imagery.


Note: You can also add these to a database like Airtable, supabase, or forward the messages thru slack. Just configure those nodes as needed.
